rct_screen_evidence: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel... Patients were stratified according to age... and the extent of metastases..."
intervention: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus docetaxel (75 mg/m² every 3 weeks for six cycles)

1.1.quote: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel..." (randomization_section)
1.1.justification: The report states that random assignment occurred but does not describe the specific random sequence generation method.

1.2.quote: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy..." (randomization_section)
1.2.justification: No details are provided about how allocation was concealed from enrolling personnel.

1.3.quote: "Baseline Characteristics... Age, race, ECOG status, volume of metastases, Gleason score, PSA level, prior treatment... are comparable between groups" (baseline_characteristics)
1.3.justification: The baseline table shows balanced groups with no substantial differences that would suggest a problem with randomization.

2.1.quote: "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone." (Methods)
2.1.justification: Participants were explicitly informed of their treatment allocation because the interventions (chemotherapy vs hormone therapy alone) are distinct and no blinding procedure is described.

2.2.quote: "We assigned men ... to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone." (Methods)
2.2.justification: Healthcare providers administering the interventions would necessarily know the allocation, as chemotherapy administration cannot be masked and no blinding of staff is reported.

3.1.quote: "All randomly assigned patients were followed and included in the primary analysis of their assigned group." (Patients section)
3.1.justification: Survival outcomes were reported for every randomized participant, with deaths enumerated and censored participants accounted for.

4.1.justification: All-cause mortality is an objective, standard outcome measured via vital status records.

4.2.quote: "Patients were enrolled... All patients provided written informed consent" (blinding_section)
4.2.justification: Death ascertainment procedures were applied identically to both arms.

4.3.quote: "The ECOG-ACRIN Statistical Center collected the data" (blinding_section)
4.3.justification: Outcome data were collected centrally and not dependent on knowledge of treatment allocation.

5.1.quote: "With each amendment, the sample size was adjusted... an intention-to-treat analysis plan was used... Kaplan-Meier estimates... Cox proportional-hazard models, stratified according to the factors described above, were used to estimate hazard ratios... An intention-to-treat analysis was conducted that included all randomly assigned patients regardless of eligibility and treatment status" (Statistical Analysis Plan)
5.1.justification: The protocol specifies an ITT, stratified Cox analysis before any outcome data were unblinded, but detailed timing of finalization is not explicit.

5.2.quote: "Overall survival was the registered primary endpoint" (Registration/Protocol section) and the result reported is the hazard ratio for overall survival.
5.2.justification: All eligible measurements for the primary outcome (overall survival) are reported; no alternative time points or definitions are presented.

5.3.quote: "Cox proportional-hazard models, stratified according to the factors described above, were used to estimate hazard ratios for time-to-event end points" (Statistical Analysis Plan)
5.3.justification: Only the pre‑specified stratified Cox model is presented; no alternative adjusted or unadjusted analyses are reported.

2.3.quote: "6 patients in the combination group did not start the assigned therapy" (Results)
2.3.justification: Deviations were limited to non‑initiation of therapy and are not described as arising from trial context.

2.6.quote: "An intention-to-treat analysis was conducted that included all randomly assigned patients regardless of eligibility and treatment status." (Statistical Analysis)
2.6.justification: All randomized participants were analyzed in their assigned groups, meeting ITT criteria.

domain_rationales.D1: Row: Any / NI / N-PN-NI -> Some concerns (concealment unclear)
domain_rationales.D2: Part1=Low (Part1 Low: awareness present/unclear but no trial-context deviations); Part2=Low (2.6=Y/PY (appropriate ITT analysis) -> Part2=Low)
